application.question: You are applying for a position as a marketing assistant at a design agency.

opening: I am writing to apply for the position of Marketing Assistant as advertised on your website.
I wish to express my interest in the Marketing Assistant role currently available at your agency.
Having seen your advertisement for a Marketing Assistant, I am writing to submit my application.
main: I have recently completed a degree in Communications and have gained relevant experience through internships.
My background in digital marketing, combined with a strong creative portfolio, makes me an ideal candidate.
Over the past two years, I have developed practical skills in content creation, campaign planning, and analytics.
details: I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my skills could benefit your team.
I am available for interview at your earliest convenience and can provide references on request.
Please find my CV attached. I look forward to the possibility of joining your creative team.
closing: I look forward to hearing from you and thank you for considering my application.
Thank you for taking the time to review my application. I hope to hear from you soon.
I am excited about the prospect of contributing to your agency and hope to discuss this further.

application.question: You are applying for a summer internship at an international hotel chain.

opening: I am writing to apply for a summer internship at your hotel, which I discovered through your company website.
I wish to express my keen interest in joining your hotel chain as a summer intern.
Having admired your hotel group's reputation for excellence, I am writing to apply for a summer placement.
main: I am currently studying Hospitality Management and am eager to gain hands-on industry experience.
My studies have equipped me with a solid understanding of customer service and hotel operations.
I have previous experience working in a customer-facing role and speak three languages fluently.
details: I am available from June to August and would be flexible regarding the specific department.
I am particularly interested in your front-of-house and events teams, though I am open to any placement.
I would be happy to discuss the details of the placement at a time that suits you.
closing: I look forward to the opportunity to contribute to your team this summer.
Thank you for considering my application. I hope to hear from you at your earliest convenience.
I would be delighted to attend an interview and provide further information as required.

request.question: You would like to request a refund for a faulty product you purchased online.

opening: I am writing to request a full refund for a product I purchased from your online store on 14th March.
I wish to formally request a refund in connection with order number 78432, placed on your website.
I am contacting you regarding a faulty item I received and would like to request compensation.
main: Upon receiving the item, I discovered that it was defective and did not match the description provided.
The product arrived damaged and is entirely unusable, despite being advertised as brand new.
The item ceased to function within 48 hours of use, which suggests a manufacturing defect.
details: I would be grateful if you could arrange a full refund to my original payment method within 14 days.
I am happy to return the item at your expense and would appreciate a prepaid returns label.
Could you please advise me on the returns procedure and the expected timeline for reimbursement?
closing: I look forward to your prompt response and a satisfactory resolution to this matter.
I trust you will deal with this matter swiftly and I thank you for your attention.
Please do not hesitate to contact me should you require any further information.

request.question: You are requesting information about an adult language course at a local college.

opening: I am writing to request further information about the adult language courses available at your college.
Having seen your advertisement for evening language classes, I am writing to enquire about enrolment.
I wish to find out more about the language programmes you offer for adult learners.
main: I am particularly interested in your Spanish evening course and would like details about the curriculum.
I am a complete beginner and would like to know which level of course would be most appropriate for me.
I have some prior experience learning French and am now looking to begin Italian from scratch.
details: Could you please provide information about course fees, start dates, and whether classes are held online or in person?
I would also like to know whether any financial assistance or instalment payment options are available.
I would appreciate details of the qualification awarded upon completion and its level of recognition.
closing: I look forward to receiving the information and hope to enrol in the near future.
Thank you for your time. I would be happy to visit the college to discuss options in person.
I look forward to your response and to potentially joining one of your courses.

complaint.question: You are complaining about a noisy construction project that has been disrupting your neighbourhood.

opening: I am writing to formally complain about the ongoing construction work on Elm Street, which has been causing significant disruption.
I wish to bring to your attention a serious issue regarding the construction project currently taking place near my home.
I am contacting you to raise a formal complaint about the noise and disruption caused by building works on our street.
main: The construction work begins as early as 6 am on weekdays and has frequently continued into the evening.
Over the past three weeks, the noise levels have made it impossible to sleep, work from home, or enjoy normal daily life.
Despite several informal requests to the site manager, the situation has not improved.
details: I would request that working hours be restricted to between 8 am and 6 pm in line with local regulations.
I would also appreciate being informed of the expected completion date and any other planned disruptions.
I expect this matter to be investigated promptly and for appropriate action to be taken.
closing: I trust that you will treat this complaint with the seriousness it deserves.
I look forward to your response and expect to be contacted within five working days.
Please do not hesitate to contact me if you require any further details about the incidents described.

complaint.question: You are complaining about poor service you received at a hotel during your holiday.

opening: I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the service I received during my recent stay at your hotel.
I wish to formally complain about several issues I encountered during my stay at your property last week.
I am contacting you to bring a number of serious concerns to your attention following my recent visit.
main: Despite booking a sea-view room, I was assigned an interior room overlooking a car park without explanation.
The room was not cleaned on two of the three nights, and my requests for fresh towels went unaddressed.
The hotel restaurant was frequently closed, and staff were unhelpful when I raised concerns at reception.
details: I would request a partial refund of 30% of the total booking cost as compensation for the inconvenience.
I expect a written response outlining what corrective measures will be taken.
I would also appreciate an assurance that these standards will be addressed before future guests arrive.
closing: I hope you will treat this complaint with the urgency it requires and respond within seven days.
I look forward to your prompt reply and a satisfactory resolution.
If I do not hear from you within a reasonable timeframe, I will escalate this matter to the relevant consumer authority.

proposal.question: You are proposing a new recycling scheme to your local council.

opening: I am writing to propose a new community recycling initiative that I believe would greatly benefit our area.
I wish to put forward a proposal for an enhanced recycling programme to be introduced in our neighbourhood.
I am contacting you to outline a scheme that I feel would significantly improve waste management in our community.
main: The current recycling facilities are inadequate, and many residents are unsure about what materials can be recycled.
Recent surveys suggest that the majority of households would participate in a more structured recycling system.
Similar schemes in neighbouring boroughs have resulted in a 40% reduction in landfill waste within twelve months.
details: I propose the introduction of colour-coded bins, a monthly collection schedule, and a public awareness campaign.
The scheme could be funded in part through a national environmental grant that your office is eligible to apply for.
I would be happy to volunteer my time to assist with the planning and rollout of the programme.
closing: I hope you will consider this proposal seriously and would welcome the chance to discuss it further.
I look forward to your response and am available to meet at your convenience.
I believe this initiative could make a genuine difference and I trust you will give it due consideration.

recommendation.question: You are recommending one of two locations for your company's annual conference.

opening: I am writing to provide my recommendation regarding the venue for this year's annual conference.
Following a review of both proposed locations, I would like to formally recommend one for your consideration.
I am writing to outline my assessment of the two venues and to recommend the most suitable option.
main: After visiting both venues, I believe the Grand Central Conference Centre offers superior facilities and transport links.
While both locations have merits, the Riverside Hotel is better equipped to accommodate our expected number of delegates.
The city-centre venue provides easier access for international attendees and a wider range of accommodation options nearby.
details: The recommended venue can accommodate up to 300 delegates and offers a fully equipped AV suite at no additional cost.
In terms of cost, the Grand Central represents better value, with a 10% early booking discount currently available.
I have already made a provisional enquiry and can confirm the venue is available on our preferred dates.
closing: I am confident this venue will ensure a successful and professional event. I am happy to discuss this further.
I would be glad to arrange a visit to the venue if required before a final decision is made.
I hope this recommendation is helpful and look forward to your feedback at your earliest convenience.

congratulate.question: Your friend has just passed their driving test and you want to congratulate them.

opening: I just heard the amazing news — you passed your driving test! I'm so thrilled for you!
I can't believe it — you actually passed! I'm so proud of you and had to write to say so.
Huge congratulations on passing your driving test! I knew you could do it!
main: I know how hard you worked for this and how nervous you were before the test.
After all those lessons and practice sessions, you absolutely deserve this.
You've been working towards this for so long, and it's brilliant to see it finally pay off.
details: Now that you have your licence, we absolutely have to plan a road trip together soon!
Seriously though, I hope you're celebrating in style — you've earned it!
Does this mean you'll be giving me lifts now? I'm only half joking!
closing: Congratulations again — I'm so happy for you! Let's catch up soon.
Can't wait to hear all about it. Let me know when you're free for a proper celebration!
Well done again. You should be really proud of yourself!

invite.question: You are inviting a friend to spend a weekend at your new flat.

opening: I've been meaning to get in touch for ages — I'd love for you to come and stay for a weekend!
Now that I've finally settled into my new flat, I'd really like to invite you to come and visit.
I know we've both been busy, but I was wondering if you'd like to come and stay with me for a few days?
main: The flat is finally looking great and I think you'd love it — it's in a brilliant location too.
I've got a proper spare room now, so you'd be totally comfortable, and there's loads to do nearby.
I've been itching to show you the place and it would be so nice to actually have time to catch up properly.
details: I was thinking maybe the weekend of the 15th? We could explore the area and have a proper catch-up.
Let me know what works for you and I'll make sure we have plenty planned — or not, if you'd rather just relax!
I could pick you up from the station if that helps, and we could go from there.
closing: I really hope you can make it — it's been too long! Let me know what you think.
Can't wait to hear from you. It would be so good to see you!
Drop me a message and we'll sort out the details. Really hope you can come!

apologise.question: You forgot your friend's birthday and are writing to apologise.

opening: I am so sorry — I can't believe I missed your birthday and I feel absolutely terrible about it.
I just wanted to write and say how genuinely sorry I am for forgetting your birthday.
I owe you a huge apology. I completely forgot your birthday and I'm so ashamed of myself.
main: There's really no good excuse — I've just had an incredibly hectic few weeks and completely lost track of the date.
I had actually put a reminder in my phone but somehow it didn't go off, though that's no excuse at all.
I know forgetting isn't something I can easily make up for, but I really am genuinely sorry.
details: I'd love to take you out for dinner to make it up to you — my treat, wherever you fancy.
I've got a little gift for you too, which I'll bring when we next meet up.
Can we please arrange something soon so I can actually celebrate you properly?
closing: I really am so sorry again and I hope you can forgive me. Happy (belated) birthday!
You're one of my favourite people and you deserved better from me. Let's sort something out soon!
Sorry again — please don't let it spoil things. Let me know when you're free!

advise.question: A friend has asked for advice on whether to move to a new city for a job opportunity.

opening: Thanks so much for reaching out — this sounds like such a big decision and I'm glad you asked for my thoughts.
I've been thinking about what you said, and I really wanted to share my honest opinion with you.
What a dilemma! I can totally see why you're finding it hard to decide, but I do have some thoughts.
main: Honestly, I think this could be a fantastic opportunity — the kind that doesn't come along very often.
From everything you've told me, the job sounds incredible and the city has so much going for it.
I know leaving is scary, but sometimes you have to take a leap and trust yourself.
details: I'd really encourage you to think about where you want to be in five years' time and whether staying would get you there.
It might be worth visiting the city first if you haven't already — that could really help you decide.
Have you thought about going for a trial period? Some employers are open to that if you ask.
closing: Whatever you decide, I'll support you — but personally, I think you should go for it!
Let me know what you decide and if you want to talk it through more, just call me anytime.
You've got this — trust your instincts and don't let fear hold you back. Good luck!

arrange.question: You are arranging a surprise birthday party for a mutual friend with another friend.

opening: I've been thinking — we absolutely have to do something special for Maya's birthday this year!
Right, I've had an idea and I need your help. What do you think about throwing Maya a surprise party?
I know her birthday is coming up and I really think we should do something memorable — are you in?
main: I was thinking we could hire out the back room at The Oak on Saturday the 22nd — it's the perfect size.
If we each invite about ten people and keep it under wraps, it could be such a brilliant surprise.
I've already checked and the venue is free that weekend, so we'd just need to get the numbers together.
details: Could you handle the decorations and I'll sort the food? We could split the cost evenly.
We'd need to make sure nobody tells her — maybe we could use a group chat and keep her out of it.
I was thinking we get everyone there for 7pm and then bring Maya in at 7.15 — what do you think?
closing: Let me know if you're up for it and we can start planning properly. So excited about this!
Give me a call when you get a chance and we can go through the details. This is going to be so fun!
Can't wait to hear what you think — I'm sure she'll absolutely love it!
